title: Thelypteris veitchii
taxonomy: Thelypteris veitchii is a species of fern within the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which includes several species of ferns. The genus Thelypteris is part of the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or wood ferns. Thelypteris veitchii is native to Asia and is often found in shaded, humid environments. Thelypteris veitchii is classified as an evergreen, deciduous fern.
uses: Thelypteris veitchii, also known as the maidenhair fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which may help in treating wounds and infections.
